reasoning: Virgin Galactic (SPCE) has approximately $867 million in cash and short-term investments as of the end of Q1 2024. The company's cash burn is around $125 million per quarter. At this burn rate, the company has roughly 7 quarters of runway, which extends through the end of 2025. Adding recent cost-cutting measures such as workforce reductions and the retiring of the VSS Unity to prioritize Delta class development, SPCE's runway comfortably stretches very close to or past the January 1, 2026 deadline. Even if cost overruns occur, as a public company, VG has the ability to conduct at-the-market offerings to raise additional marginal capital. Richard Branson's refusal to invest further is a bearish signal, but the simple math of cash-on-hand versus burn rate dictates that bankruptcy before 2026 is unlikely unless catastrophic unforeseen expenses arise. The community forecast of 13% is reasonable, but as time passes and the window narrows, I estimate it closer to 11%.
